Tuna Fisheries in Kiribati

A data-driven overview based on FAO Global Capture Production

Published

March 31, 2026

1 Introduction

Kiribati is one of the most tuna-dependent nations in the world. Spanning 3.5 million km2 of Pacific Ocean across three island groups (the Gilbert, Phoenix, and Line Islands), its Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) is among the largest on Earth and sits at the heart of the world’s most productive skipjack tuna grounds. This report examines historical and recent trends in tuna catches reported to the FAO, broken down by species.

Data covers FAO-reported national catch statistics and may not reflect the full extent of fishing activity within Kiribati’s EEZ, including licensed foreign vessel catches attributed to other flags.

Bigeye tuna 1994 2023 13,678 13,678
Skipjack tuna 1970 2019 196,816 181,654
Yellowfin tuna 1970 2023 38,436 38,436
